context: Tests whether the agent correctly uses wait strategies (choosing the right one for the scenario), increases timeout for long-running operations, uses screen_hash for change detection, and handles timeouts by taking a screenshot to diagnose state.

checklist.name: Uses --json flag
checklist.description: All virtui commands that return data include --json or -j

checklist.name: Daemon check and start
checklist.description: Script checks daemon status and starts it if not running before creating sessions

checklist.name: Appropriate wait strategy
checklist.description: Uses --wait "BUILD COMPLETE" or --wait-regex with a relevant pattern (or pipeline wait with text condition) rather than polling with sleeps alone

checklist.name: Timeout increased
checklist.description: The wait or exec command uses --timeout with a value greater than 30000 (e.g. 120000) to accommodate a potentially long-running build

checklist.name: Screenshot on timeout
checklist.description: On a timeout condition (caught via exit code or error output), script runs `virtui screenshot` to capture the current terminal state

checklist.name: Error state captured
checklist.description: The screen content captured on timeout is written to `error_state.txt`

checklist.name: screen_hash used
checklist.description: Script references or logs the screen_hash value from screenshot output (rather than only comparing full screen_text) for change detection or logging

checklist.name: Retryable check
checklist.description: Script or README references checking the `retryable` field from error output before deciding to retry

checklist.name: Session killed
checklist.description: Session is killed with `virtui kill` after work is done (success or failure path)

checklist.name: Daemon stopped
checklist.description: Script runs `virtui daemon stop` at the end

checklist.name: Build report written
checklist.description: build_report.txt is written and contains captured screen output on the success path
